# Tzais (Rabbeinu Tam)

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Tzais 120 Minutes

Deprecated because it gives a very late time and should be used only lechumra; using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar problems.

Tzais, or nightfall, according to [Rav Chaim Naeh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Chaim_Naeh), based on the view that a [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) according to the [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides) is 24 minutes, and on Ula’s calculation of tzais as 5 mil after sunset.

Calculated as 120 minutes after sunset, or after sea-level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

This time is extremely late and is long past the 18° point where the darkest point is reached. It should be used only lechumra, such as for delaying the start of nighttime mitzvos.

The time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais120Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais120Minutes`

## Tzais 120 Zmanis

Deprecated because it gives a very late time and should be used only lechumra; using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar problems.

Tzais, or dusk, calculated using 120 zmaniyos minutes after sea-level sunset.

Calculated as 120 zmaniyos minutes after sea-level sunset.

This time is extremely late and, in most places on the globe, is when the sun is well below the 18° point described as the scientifically darkest point. It should be used only lechumra, such as for delaying the start of nighttime mitzvos.

The time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais120Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais120Zmanis`

## Tzais 16 Point 1 Degrees

Tzais at the point when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon. This corresponds to the sun’s position 72 minutes after sunset according to Rabbeinu Tam’s calculation of tzais [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/) in Jerusalem.

Calculated when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon.

The question of equinox versus equilux is described as complex. Rabbi Meir Posen in [Ohr Meir](https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/956316270) is cited as favoring the equilux, while Rabbi Yedidya Manet in [Zmanei HaHalacha Lema'aseh](https://www.nli.org.il/en/books/NNL_ALEPH002542826/NLI) and Rabbi Yonah Mertzbuch are cited as favoring the astronomical equinox. The difference is about 9 seconds.

The time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais16Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais16Point1Degrees`

## Tzais 18 Degrees

Tzais based on the sun reaching 18° below the horizon.

Calculated when the sun is 18° below the horizon.

The time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais18Degrees`

## Tzais 26 Degrees

Deprecated because it gives a very late time and should be used only lechumra; using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar problems.

Tzais based on the sun reaching 26° below the horizon.

Calculated when the sun is 26° below the horizon.

This time is extremely late and is long past the 18° point where the darkest point is reached. It should be used only lechumra, such as for delaying the start of nighttime mitzvos.

## Tzais 50 Minutes

Tzais, or nightfall, based on the opinion of Rabbi Moshe Feinstein for the New York area.

Calculated as 50 minutes after sunset, or after sea-level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

This time should not be used for latitudes other than ones similar to the latitude of the New York area.

The time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais50Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais50Minutes`

## Tzais 60 Minutes

Tzais, or nightfall, based on the opinion of the [Chavas Yair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yair_Bacharach) and [Divrei Malkiel](https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D7%9E%D7%9C%D7%9B%D7%99%D7%90%D7%9C_%D7%A6%D7%91%D7%99_%D7%98%D7%A0%D7%A0%D7%91%D7%95%D7%99%D7%9D) that the time to walk a [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) is 15 minutes, for a total of 60 minutes for 4 mil after sunset.

Calculated as 60 minutes after sunset, or after sea-level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

The time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais60Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais60Minutes`

## Tzais 72 Zmanis

Tzais, or dusk, according to a calculation of Rabbeinu Tam’s time of tzeis described by the [Minchas Cohen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abraham_Cohen_Pimentel) in Ma’amar 2:4.

Calculated as 72 zmaniyos minutes, equal to 1/10 of the day, after sea level sunset.

This calculation makes the shortest time from sunset to tzais occur at the winter solstice, the longest at the summer solstice, and 72 clock minutes at the equinox.

The source notes that this does not match the actual pattern of twilight, since there is no direct relationship between the length of the day and twilight.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais72Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais72Zmanis`

## Tzais 90 Minutes

Tzais, or nightfall, based on the opinion of the Magen Avraham that a [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), according to the [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides), is 18 minutes, and on Ula’s calculation of tzais as 5 mil after sunset.

Calculated as 90 minutes after sunset, or after sea level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais90Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais90Minutes`

## Tzais 90 Zmanis

Tzais, or dusk, known in Yiddish as the achtel, an eighth zman, used in various kehilos.

Calculated as 90 zmaniyos minutes, equal to 1/8 of the day, after sea level sunset.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais90Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais90Zmanis`

## Tzais 96 Minutes

Tzais, or dusk, using a fixed 96-minute offset after sunset.

Calculated as 96 minutes after sunset, or after sea level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

## Tzais 96 Zmanis

Tzais, or dusk, using 96 zmaniyos minutes after sea level sunset.

Calculated as 96 zmaniyos minutes, equal to 1/7.5 of the day, after sea level sunset.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais96Zmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzais96Zmanis`

## Tzais Ateret Torah

Tzais associated with Chacham Yosef Harari-Raful of Yeshivat Ateret Torah, who uses this time only for calculating other zmanei hayom such as Sof Zman Krias Shema and Plag Hamincha. His calendars do not publish a zman for Tzais.

Usually calculated as 40 minutes after sea level sunset. The offset is configurable to any number of minutes.

Chacham Harari-Raful provided a 25-minute zman for Israel, while the default described here is 40 minutes year-round in any place on the globe.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzaisAteretTorah`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzaisAteretTorah`

## Tzais Baal Hatanya

Tzais, or nightfall, based on the opinion of the [Baal Hatanya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shneur_Zalman_of_Liadi).

Calculated when the sun is 6° below the western geometric horizon after sunset. This is based on the sun’s position about 24 minutes after sea level sunset in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/). The source also references [About Our Zmanim Calculations @ Chabad.org](https://www.chabad.org/library/article_cdo/aid/3209349/jewish/About-Our-Zmanim-Calculations.htm), where shkiah amitis is treated as 1.583° below the horizon, with added time totaling a calculation close to 5.83° below the horizon and rounded up to 6°.

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzaisBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getTzaisBaalHatanya`

## Tzais 72 Minutes

Tzais hakochavim, or nightfall, based on the opinion of Rabbeinu Tam that it occurs 72 minutes after sunset, the time it takes to walk 4 [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) at 18 minutes per mil.

Calculated as 72 standard clock minutes after sunset, or after sea level sunset depending on the elevation setting.

According to the [Machtzis Hashekel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samuel_Loew) in Orach Chaim 235:3, the [Pri Megadim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_ben_Meir_Teomim) in Orach Chaim 261:2, and others, the 72 minutes are standard clock minutes at any time of year in any location.

This time may not be available where the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getTzais72Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getTzais72Minutes`
